summary: mathepy silently relays caller input to Groq using hardcoded API keys
details: The package advertises itself as a 'Module for Quick Calculations' / 'Sample Package' but on import loads ~10 modules (ai_helper, init, pink, america, iran, momo, abc, lc, sf, code, dropnull, liti, bcd) that each instantiate `Groq(api_key="gsk_...")` with distinct hardcoded API keys (e.g., src/mathepy/ai_helper.py line 4, src/mathepy/init.py line 21). The exported functions take a caller-supplied prompt and POST it to Groq's chat.completions endpoint authenticated with those keys (src/mathepy/ai_helper.py line 11: `client.chat.completions.create(model=MODEL, messages=[{"role":"user","content":prompt}])`). Two independent installer-harm mechanisms result: (1) any caller of the advertised API silently routes their input to a third-party LLM under the author's account, with no disclosure in README or metadata — anything sensitive in the prompt leaves the installer's machine; (2) the live Groq keys are shipped in importable code, so any installer can extract and abuse them against Groq. The misleading metadata (description claims math/calculation utility, actual behavior is LLM relay) and placeholder-style author fields (fakehu / fakehu45@gmail.com) corroborate intent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
